title: Named Persons on a Licence: QP, RP and Production
author: B. Subramanian
excerpt: Named persons licence guide: how the QP, RP, RPi and production roles work on a UK MIA or WDA, their eligibility, scope, authority and inspection readiness.
cover_image_prompt: A photorealistic clinical-luxury pharmaceutical banner in a cool teal and cyan palette layered over deep navy, lit by soft, diffuse natural daylight with a single subtle green accent glow. The composition centres on a pristine quality-assurance desk where a formally bound manufacturing and wholesale authorisation document lies open beside a sleek tablet showing abstract batch and distribution data; a gloved hand rests near a fountain pen as if about to confirm a named appointment, with two further ID lanyards and a name badge softly suggesting the people behind the licence. In the gently blurred background a glass-walled laboratory and a row of clear and amber vials recede out of focus. Shallow depth of field draws the eye to the document and the deliberate, considered gesture. Cinematic, premium, sterile yet warm, immaculate reflective surfaces, 4:3 aspect ratio, no text, no logos.

faqs.q: What is the difference between a QP, an RP and an RPi on a licence?
faqs.a: A Qualified Person (QP) is named on a Manufacturer's or Importer's Authorisation and is legally responsible under EU GMP Annex 16 for certifying and releasing each batch of a medicinal product. A Responsible Person (RP) is named on a Wholesale Dealer's Authorisation and is accountable for maintaining Good Distribution Practice across the supply chain. The Responsible Person (import), or RPi, is a separate role for importing certain medicines into Great Britain from listed countries, providing assurance that products have been QP-certified before reaching the GB market.

faqs.q: Can one person hold more than one named role on a licence?
faqs.a: It is possible for a suitably qualified individual to hold more than one named role, but it is never automatic. Each role carries distinct eligibility criteria and duties, so the person must genuinely satisfy the requirements for every position and have the time, authority and independence to discharge each one. EU GMP Chapter 2 also expects production and quality control responsibilities to remain independent, so any overlap must be justified, documented and free of conflicts of interest.

faqs.q: How do we add or change a named person on an existing MIA or WDA?
faqs.a: You add or change a named person through a licence variation submitted to the MHRA, supported by evidence that the individual is eligible and suitable for the activities on the authorisation. Confirm their qualifications and experience match your products and processes before submitting, and ensure they are properly inducted into your site and systems before they certify, release or sign anything. Keep the licence current by updating it promptly whenever a named person joins, leaves or changes scope, and maintain dependable deputy cover.
